top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: brand-intelligence-lab
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Multiple labs identified untapped sponsorship and endorsement opportunities (4 equipment categories with zero brand detection, 175 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences). This deserves dedicated infrastructure to map, track, and monetize brand-athlete-product relationships systematically across time.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab.opportunity_scout (sponsorship whitespace + endorsement intelligence), general-purpose.wildcard (partner with adjacent platforms), pickle-idea-lab.wildcard (JOOLA data exchange)
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py brand-intelligence-lab — then customize config. Suggested researchers: partnership_strategist, sponsorship_mapper, longitudinal_analyst

proposal.rationale: Multiple labs identified untapped sponsorship and endorsement opportunities (4 equipment categories with zero brand detection, 175 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences). This deserves dedicated infrastructure to map, track, and monetize brand-athlete-product relationships systematically across time.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab.opportunity_scout (sponsorship whitespace + endorsement intelligence), general-purpose.wildcard (partner with adjacent platforms), pickle-idea-lab.wildcard (JOOLA data exchange)
proposal.business_context: Build a real-time brand-athlete-equipment graph that converts sponsorship whitespace into partnership RFPs, then license insights back to brands and facilities as a predictive endorsement platform.

draft_config.description: Multiple labs identified untapped sponsorship and endorsement opportunities (4 equipment categories with zero brand detection, 175 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences). This deserves dedicated infrastructure to map, track, and monetize brand-athlete-product relationships systematically across time.
draft_config.business_context: Build a real-time brand-athlete-equipment graph that converts sponsorship whitespace into partnership RFPs, then license insights back to brands and facilities as a predictive endorsement platform.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Brand intelligence requires multi-source data integration (social, web, news APIs), sentiment analysis models, and competitive datasets—achievable in 30 days only as a basic MVP, but market needs sophisticated analysis to justify a new lab.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Evidence is circumstantial—identifies gaps (4 categories with zero brand detection) but doesn't validate that a new lab structure would fill them better than expanding existing labs. Would need: (a) baseline comparison showing brand-intelligence-lab would outperform general-purpose on these 175 pairs, (b) evidence that structural separation (not just task assignment) drives better sponsorship discovery, (c) cost-benefit vs. adding brand-detection prompts to current labs.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Score (0.321) is low but finding still proposed. No analysis of why current labs missed these opportunities—could be intentional filtering, task design, or data gaps rather than missing perspective. Also assumes 'untapped' = valuable; needs market validation that these 175 pairs have actual partnership potential.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: brand-intelligence-lab'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py brand-intelligence-lab — then customize config. Suggested researchers: partnership_strategist, sponsorship_mapper, longitudinal_analyst

top_strategic.title: Lab 'general-purpose' is 53% 'wildcard_idea' — blind to execution risk assessment
top_strategic.evidence_summary: 'general-purpose' findings are 53% in category 'wildcard_idea' (8/15). This lab may be missing perspectives on: execution risk assessment, market sizing. A new researcher persona could fill this gap.
top_strategic.recommended_action: Add a researcher that focuses on execution risk assessment to 'general-purpose'
top_strategic.plausibility_note: A 53% wildcard composition with unknown execution risks means the founder is flying blind on feasibility, data access, regulatory compliance, and API availability—too much uncertainty to prototype confidently in 30 days.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is sound pattern recognition. 53% concentration in one category (8/15) is statistically notable and correctly flags a gap. Would strengthen with: (a) comparison to baseline wildcard% in other labs, (b) explicit list of what execution-risk findings would look like (to verify absence), (c) whether this is a *lab design flaw* or appropriate for general-purpose's mandate.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes wildcard ≠ execution-aware, but high-creativity labs might *intentionally* deprioritize risk assessment. No signal whether general-purpose lab was *asked* to assess execution risk or whether it should have volunteered that perspective unprompted.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Lab 'general-purpose' is 53% 'wildcard_idea' — bli'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Add a researcher that focuses on execution risk assessment to 'general-purpose'

top_data.title: Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-idea-lab: 'Sell the Idea Lab itself'
top_data.evidence_summary: Lab 'general-purpose' (wildcard) found 'Sell the Idea Lab itself'. Lab 'pickle-idea-lab' (wildcard) independently found 'Sell the Idea Lab itself — every facility gets their own AI research team'. Similarity: 0.495. When multiple labs converge on a theme, it's a much stronger signal.
top_data.recommended_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

raw_data.finding_b: Sell the Idea Lab itself — every facility gets their own AI research team

multi_model_check.change_mind: Score (0.974) is justified—independent convergence is strong evidence. However, this proves *agreement*, not *validity*. Would change mind if: (a) either lab acknowledged they'd seen the other's finding (would indicate signal copying, not true convergence), (b) they derived it via different reasoning paths (increases confidence), (c) external validation that 'selling the lab' is actually viable.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: High convergence score may reflect **shared bias** rather than truth—both labs might be drawn to meta-level ideas or both lack market-validation rigor. No check for whether this idea survived past similar vetting before.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-id'?
follow_up.proposed_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

top_data.title: Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs
top_data.evidence_summary: The finding category 'wildcard_idea' is being produced by multiple labs: {'general-purpose': 8, 'pickle-idea-lab': 10}. This suggests a cross-cutting pattern type.
top_data.recommended_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Identifies real overlap (8+10=18 wildcard findings) but correlation ≠ problem. This is only concerning if: (a) the labs were meant to be independent/complementary (are they?), (b) wildcard_idea is being *over*-produced relative to other categories, (c) the finding itself is actionable (should we merge labs? add guardrails? is this expected?). Without those frames, this is observation, not insight.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No baseline—what's the expected wildcard rate? Are 2 labs producing wildcard ideas *more* than 1 lab would? The signal could indicate healthy exploratory diversity or poorly-calibrated prompts; can't tell from counts alone.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

top_data.title: No lab subscriptions configured
top_data.evidence_summary: No labs have declared subscriptions yet. Add `subscriptions: ["lab-id:researcher"]` to a lab's config to enable cross-lab signal routing.
top_data.recommended_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Factually correct and actionable. No mind-change needed—this is configuration documentation, not a falsifiable claim.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Finding assumes subscriptions are *necessary* for useful cross-lab signals, but Findings 3 & 4 show emergent patterns *without* subscriptions. This may actually indicate organic signal quality is already working. Recommending subscriptions is reasonable but finding doesn't justify *why* it's urgent.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No lab subscriptions configured'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is sound pattern recognition. 53% concentration in one category (8/15) is statistically notable and correctly flags a gap. Would strengthen with: (a) comparison to baseline wildcard% in other labs, (b) explicit list of what execution-risk findings would look like (to verify absence), (c) whether this is a *lab design flaw* or appropriate for general-purpose's mandate.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes wildcard ≠ execution-aware, but high-creativity labs might *intentionally* deprioritize risk assessment. No signal whether general-purpose lab was *asked* to assess execution risk or whether it should have volunteered that perspective unprompted.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Lab 'general-purpose' is 53% 'wildcard_idea' — bli'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Add a researcher that focuses on execution risk assessment to 'general-purpose'
